mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.11% is at the effective zero lower bound, signaling an aggressively accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.11% near zero lower bound as of 2018-11
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is non-positive or not significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.10) during any 2-year rolling window where FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 7.6% is well above the natural rate, indicating significant labor market slack that historically precedes economic recovery, fiscal/monetary stimulus, and rising corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE > 7.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 421147 million dollars reflect strong consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns as the market prices in sustained demand growth.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=421147 as of 2018-10, near cycle highs
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive in 3 consecutive months but the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the fed funds rate is below 1% and unemployment exceeds 6%, the combination of extreme monetary accommodation and labor market slack creates a powerful tailwind for equities, as policy is expected to remain easy until full employment is reached, boosting forward returns.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is non-positive when both conditions hold over any 2-year rolling window, or if the joint signal's IC is lower than the sum of individual ICs.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (real GDP growth positive), rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing output and capacity utilization, which signals accelerating economic activity and supports rising corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If during quarters with positive GDP growth the rank correlation between INDPRO 1-month change and S&P 500 forward 20-day return falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the relationship reverses in a recession.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 853 thousand units are below the critical 900 thousand threshold, signaling weakness in the housing sector that historically precedes broader economic downturns and drags on GDP, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive when HOUST < 900 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal's hit ratio is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: When both employment growth (PAYEMS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerate simultaneously, the economy risks overheating, prompting expectations of tighter Fed policy and higher discount rates, which depresses equity prices and produces negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series trending upward as of late 2018
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances when both triggers fire over any 1-year rolling window, or the joint IC is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 13746.2 billion dollars reflects the overall size of the economy; a rising level indicates aggregate demand strength, which directly supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=13746.2 (Q3 2018), highest ever at decision time
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is non-positive in more than one-third of quarters with positive GDP growth over any 5-year rolling window, or rank IC < 0.05.
